circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track designed by Hermann Tilke on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi, has served as the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. The circuit represents the second Formula One venue established in the Middle East, following Bahrain's circuit and preceding those in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park Circuit is a street track situated around Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, hosting the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year alongside supporting racing series. The venue maintains FIA Grade 1 circuit status.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit hosted the Austrian Grand Prix during two separate eras: first from 1970 to 1987 under its original name, then again from 1997 to 2003 following a redesign and shortening of the track.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital city, stretching 6. 003 kilometers to rank as the fourth-longest venue in Formula One. The circuit hosted its first F1 race as the 2016 European Grand Prix, before becoming a regular fixture on the calendar with the Azerbaijan Grand Prix beginning in 2017.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit spans 5. 412 km and hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One Grand Prix that year. The FIA Grade 1-licensed facility accommodates various motorsport events including GP2 racing, drag racing, and 24-hour endurance competitions, while temporarily featuring Australian V8 Supercars between 2006 and 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657-kilometer all-purpose racing venue features a balanced mix of extended straightaways and varied corner configurations that test diverse driving skills. The circuit holds FIA Grade 1 accreditation and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: This 7. 004 km Belgian circuit in Wallonia has been central to Formula One racing since hosting the sport's debut season in 1950, though motorsport events there date back to 1925. The venue currently stages the Belgian Grand Prix annually, with its consecutive hosting only interrupted by cancellations in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve spans 4. 361 kilometres and serves as the permanent home of the Canadian Grand Prix in Formula One. Beyond its primary role in international motorsport's pinnacle series, the venue has accommodated various other racing championships including sportscar and open-wheel competitions throughout its history.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, has hosted the Chinese Grand Prix intermittently since 2004, with a hiatus between 2020 and 2023 before resuming in 2024. Beyond Formula One racing, the venue has expanded its motorsport calendar to include electric racing competitions beginning in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near Northamptonshire in England, Silverstone is the traditional venue for the British Grand Prix, having first staged the event in 1948 and hosting the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After sharing hosting duties with Aintree and Brands Hatch between 1955 and 1986, the circuit became the permanent home of Britain's Formula One grand prix from 1987 onwards.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest hosted Formula One's maiden Grand Prix behind the Iron Curtain in 1986, making it a historically significant venue. Built in just eight months starting from late 1985, the 4. 381 km circuit was constructed outside the city near a highway after plans for a Monaco-style street circuit in Budapest's largest park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Monza north of Milan, the Autodromo Nazionale is a 5. 793 km Italian racing venue built in 1922 as mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built circuit. The track regularly hosts the Italian Grand Prix, which has taken place there continuously since 1949 except for a 1980 closure during renovation, and earned its "Temple of Speed" nickname through its characteristic long straights and fast corners.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, accommodating 155,000 spectators. The track has gained prominence as a premier destination for Formula One and Super Formula racing championships.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, adjacent to Las Vegas proper, following the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its surrounding thoroughfares. The layout weaves past iconic venues and casinos such as Caesars Palace, Bellagio, Paris Las Vegas, and the MSG Sphere, creating a challenging urban racing environment through the heart of the city's entertainment district.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit honoring two Mexican racing drivers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom perished in motorsport accidents. The venue has returned to Formula One's calendar since 2015, hosting the Mexican Grand Prix on its current layout, though it previously held the race during two earlier periods using a different configuration before 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile layout with 19 corners that produces average qualifying speeds near 139 mph. Designed by specialist Formula One architects for the 2022 season onwards, the track hosts the Miami Grand Prix as part of the championship calendar.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco stretches 3. 337 kilometers through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, encircling Monaco's harbour. Though officially named after the circuit itself, it is widely known as Monte Carlo due to its location primarily within that district of the principality.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that rejoined the Formula One calendar in 2021 to host the revived Dutch Grand Prix. The venue's partnership with the sport concludes in 2026.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit, positioned north of Doha in Qatar, is a 5. 419-kilometer track featuring a 1. 068-kilometer main straight and holds FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km circuit in São Paulo, Brazil, originally opened in 1940 and renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his 1977 death. The counterclockwise track features supporting facilities including a kart circuit named after Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street track situated along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea coastline that has served as the venue for the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since 2021. The circuit also began hosting a Formula E event in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's Marina Bay district, incorporating the Downtown Core and Kallang planning areas into its layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km Spanish circuit features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for racing machinery. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the permanent home of Formula One's United States Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the track hosts multiple racing series including NASCAR, MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, along with having previously accommodated various other motorsport competitions.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British racing driver who currently represents Thailand in Formula One, competing for the Williams team.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Fernando Alonso, a Spanish driver for Aston Martin, stands as a two-time Formula One World Drivers' Champion after his consecutive titles with Renault in 2005 and 2006, accumulating 32 Grand Prix victories across his 23-season career. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, winning the 2018–19 FIA World Endurance Championship, two Le Mans 24-hour races with Toyota, and the 2019 Daytona 24 hours, making him the sole competitor to claim both the F1 drivers' title and the world endurance drivers' crown.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli represents Mercedes on the Formula One grid, having secured five Grand Prix victories following his entry into the sport in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British motorsport competitor who currently races for Haas in the Formula One championship.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian racing driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races in Formula One for Audi, competing at the highest level of motorsport.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Bottas is a Finnish Formula One driver currently racing for Cadillac. The veteran competitor has secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship during his time with Mercedes and has claimed ten race victories over his 13-season career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently competes in Formula One racing for the Alpine team, representing his nation in the sport's highest category.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian driver currently serving as a reserve driver for Haas in Formula One while competing in European endurance racing. He has participated in seven Grand Prix events during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The driver achieved a Grand Prix victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while competing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar is a French-Algerian racing driver who races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One, competing under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: British driver Lewis Hamilton currently races for Ferrari in Formula One, having secured seven world championship titles that equal Michael Schumacher's record. He holds multiple all-time records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One. Beyond single-seaters, he achieved endurance racing success by winning the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 alongside Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand racing driver currently competing in Formula One for Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc is a Monégasque driver competing for Ferrari in Formula One, having achieved nine race victories over his career spanning nine seasons. He finished as runner-up in the 2022 drivers' championship while driving for the Italian team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver who previously competed in Formula One across two separate periods between 2014 and 2024. Currently, he races in the World Endurance Championship for BMW through WRT and competes part-time in NASCAR's top series with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship. The achievement came after he secured 11 Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently competing for Haas. The driver achieved a significant career milestone by winning the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while racing for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from 15 seasons in the sport. He finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while driving for Red Bull and has claimed six Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian Formula One driver currently competing for McLaren, with nine race victories accumulated over his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed across 14 seasons in Formula One between 2011 and 2024, claiming eight Grand Prix victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, where he has secured seven Grand Prix victories throughout his career spanning eight seasons.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz Vázquez de Castro currently races for Williams in Formula One, where he has accumulated four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver currently racing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition. Following a brief Formula One tenure spanning 2023 to 2024, he has transitioned to endurance racing, having previously competed in the IMSA SportsCar Championship's LMP2 class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor who currently drives for Aston Martin in Formula One racing.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda, a Japanese driver, has been involved in Formula One racing since 2021, currently holding a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch-Belgian driver competes for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Across his 12-season career, Verstappen has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is China's sole Formula One competitor to date, having raced in the sport between 2022 and 2024. He currently holds a reserve driver position with Cadillac in the championship.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has operated as Alpine since 2021, continuing Renault's works team status until the manufacturer's 2025 departure from the sport. Competition under the Alpine banner has yielded a single victory, courtesy of Esteban Ocon's triumph at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, while the squad currently relies on Mercedes power units and fields Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto as its drivers.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has competed in Formula One under multiple guises throughout its history, with the current iteration operating as AMR GP Limited and racing under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished team, participating in every World Championship season since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad, colloquially known as "the Prancing Horse" for its iconic emblem, remains a cornerstone of motorsport competition.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas's American-licensed F1 outfit debuted in 2016 and has competed continuously since its establishment in 2014. Leadership transitioned from founding principal Guenther Steiner to Ayao Komatsu in early 2024.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Peter Sauber's Swiss engineering outfit, established in 1970, distinguished itself in endurance racing before joining Formula One in 1993, where it competed for over three decades as an independent constructor. The team secured notable victories including the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours and accumulated significant racing experience across multiple iterations before its acquisition by Audi in 2024 and subsequent exit from the championship in 2025, ranking fourth among the longest-serving constructors by race entries.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that competes under the McLaren Mastercard banner. The team operates as part of the broader McLaren Group, which is owned by Bahrain's Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: The Mercedes-AMG Petronas team, operating from Brackley under a German licence, represents Mercedes-Benz's long-standing involvement in Formula One dating back to 1954 as both constructor and engine supplier. From January 2022, ownership became tripartite among Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and Toto Wolff in equal shares. The team carries the historic "Silver Arrows" designation associated with Mercedes' racing heritage.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that began competing in 2024 as Red Bull GmbH's second factory team, operating from its Faenza headquarters alongside facilities in Milton Keynes. The squad represents one of two Red Bull-owned entries on the grid, functioning as a sister team to the primary Red Bull Racing operation.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team that launched in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team structure, alongside the main Red Bull Racing outfit. Based in Faenza with operations in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as both a racing team and constructor within the sport's regulatory framework.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence from a British base, has been a prominent Formula One outfit since its 2005 establishment as part of Red Bull GmbH's motorsport portfolio. The team was led by Christian Horner throughout its first two decades before Laurent Mekies took the helm in 2025. Red Bull GmbH also fields Racing Bulls as its second Formula One entry.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, following the collapse of Williams's previous racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in 2026 as Atlassian Williams F1 Team and has established itself as a significant presence in the sport's constructors' championship history.
